commit.message:
docs: cell-zero.cell sketch — metacircular evaluation kernel in Cell

A concrete (non-executable) sketch of cell-zero written as a Cell
program. Demonstrates:
- 6-phase evaluation loop: read → check → pick → evaluate → claims → decide
- Oracle checking via claim cells (spawn-claims → check-claims)
- Bottom handling (⊥? handlers as graph operations)
- The eval-loop as a spawner (⊢⊢) that never terminates
- Metacircular property: cell-zero can evaluate itself

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: add bottom propagation semantics to computational model

Bottom (⊥) is the absence of a value, not a special value. ⊥
propagation falls out naturally from graph evaluation: cells with
absent inputs are never ready, so their outputs are also absent.

⊥? handlers are bottom-lifting functions that provide values where
none exist, breaking the propagation chain. No special ⊥ machinery
needed — it's all graph evaluation rules.

Connects to domain theory: frozen set as lattice, monotonicity
as upward movement, handlers as lifting functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: update v0.1 spec with fusion model and oracle-as-cell

Execution model: non-termination by design, tentative outputs,
claim cells, freeze/retry protocol. References Lean proofs and
computational model doc.

Oracle system: oracles are cells (claim cells spawned by cell-zero).
Retry is graph rewrite, not state mutation. Tentative output lives
in claim cells, original cell stays unfrozen until oracles pass.

Fix dashboard auth race condition, stale token cache, and channel save 422

- Register auth:loggedIn handler before window.auth.init() so the event
  isn't missed; replace fragile 500ms setTimeout with synchronous fallback
- Invalidate session KV cache after Discord token refresh so subsequent
  requests read fresh tokens from DB instead of stale cached ones
- Stop invalidating session when refresh lock is hit by concurrent requests
- Send null instead of 'none' for channel_id when disabling, fixing Zod
  discordSnowflake validation failure (422 Unprocessable Content)

Fix write page blank after execution blocks were removed from manifests

The shell's registerManifestExtensions checked manifest.execution?.mode
=== 'native' before registering views and loading components. After
removing execution blocks from manifests (to fix production install
failures), no views or panels were registered — causing a blank write
page for newly installed bobbins.

Default to native when execution block is absent since all first-party
bobbins are native. Also update generate-native-view-map.ts and
lint-bobbins.ts to match.

Fix login spinning forever when profile row is missing

Root cause: Users who signed up before the profiles table was created
have no profile row. After signIn succeeds, the auth state listener
fetches the profile, gets null, and Login.tsx never redirects because
it waits for user && profile.

Fixes:
- AuthContext: auto-create profile row if it doesn't exist (PGRST116
  error = no rows found). Uses user_metadata for full_name and role.
- Login.tsx: add 5-second timeout that resets spinner and shows error
  if profile never loads
- Both changes ensure login works for users created before migrations

commit.message:
Remove resource blocking — CF challenge needs its own assets to pass

The page.route() interceptor was blocking images/fonts/media which
Cloudflare's JS challenge may need to complete its browser verification.
Without these resources the challenge silently fails and never redirects
to the actual scorecard. On Pro/Fluid Compute memory is not a concern.

commit.message:
Fix shredder claim race with SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED

Previous claim used SELECT then UPDATE with a race window between
them, causing multiple workers to claim and process the same staging
rows. Replaced with atomic SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED pattern —
each worker exclusively locks its rows before marking them processing.
Eliminates duplicate work and deadlocks from concurrent claims.

commit.message:
docs: extend computational model — semantic computation theory, cell-zero sketch

Adds sections on: what semantic computation is (not a Turing machine),
the generate-and-check model (NP analogy with oracle hierarchy),
why you don't need to formalize "reasonable," a concrete sketch of
what cell-zero.cell might look like, and the crystallization spectrum.

Also covers: proof-carrying as paradigmatic fusion, why Cell isn't a
workflow engine, formal connections (OTMs, KPN, lambda calculus).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Seqlock rollout guard (new):

  for the rollout guard suite

Seqlock reader/writer contract (updates):

  additional retry budget and incumbent fallback documentation

Extension host guardplane replay (hardening):

  variable support, allowing replay commands with spaces to be passed
  via FE_GUARDPLANE_REPLAY_COMMAND_ENCODED with sentinel-based space
  encoding, falling back to FE_GUARDPLANE_REPLAY_COMMAND or default

commit.message:
feat: add 6 new engine subsystem modules with integration tests

Introduces six new runtime subsystem modules to the franken-engine crate,
expanding the engine's capability surface for law promotion, metadata
optimization, JSX lowering, resource certificate consumption, semantic
canonical basis computation, and trace fusion.

New modules:
- law_promotion_pack.rs: Law promotion pack assembly and validation for
  policy-driven runtime transformations
- optimized_metadata_substrate.rs: Optimized metadata substrate layer
  providing efficient metadata storage and retrieval for engine internals
- react_jsx_lowering.rs: React JSX lowering pass that transforms JSX
  AST nodes into franken-engine IR, supporting component resolution and
  element construction
- resource_certificate_consumer.rs: Resource certificate consumer that
  validates and applies certificate-based resource authorization
- semantic_canonical_basis.rs: Semantic canonical basis computation for
  deterministic normalization of engine semantic representations
- trace_fusion.rs: Trace fusion engine that merges and optimizes runtime
  execution traces for performance analysis and replay

All modules registered in lib.rs. Three new integration test suites
added for optimized_metadata_substrate, semantic_canonical_basis, and
trace_fusion modules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet <claude-sonnet-4-6@noreply.anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

execution/stagedsync: enable deferred commitment updates for parallel fork validation (#19749)

## Problem

`SetDeferCommitmentUpdates` was restricted to serial mode (`!parallel`)
during fork (re-org) validation. The restriction existed because the
parallel executor produced intermittent trie root mismatches when
deferred updates were enabled.

Root cause of those mismatches: `applyStorageChanges` used
`blockOriginStorage[key]` (block-start value) as the skip criterion for
`WriteAccountStorage`. This caused MDBX to hold stale intermediate
values after certain intra-block storage patterns, which propagated into
trie root calculations, producing non-deterministic mismatches depending
on execution order.

That underlying bug is fixed in #19748.

## Fix

Remove the `!parallel` guard so both serial and parallel fork validation
benefit from deferred commitment updates. Deferred updates batch trie
root calculations to block boundaries rather than recomputing after
every transaction, significantly reducing re-org validation overhead in
parallel mode.

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify `make erigon` builds cleanly  
- [ ] Run `go test ./execution/stagedsync/...`
- [ ] Parallel fork validation no longer produces trie root mismatches
- [ ] Re-org handling is faster under parallel execution
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Mark Holt <erigon@dev-bm-e3-ethmainnet-n4.erigon.io>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: Cell computational model — fusion language foundations

Captures the theoretical foundation of Cell: a language that requires
both classical and semantic computation to execute. Defines cell-zero
as a metacircular kernel (.cell file), explains oracle-as-cell design,
tentative state model, and substrate independence.

Key insights from design session:
- Cell-zero is a cell, not a special runtime
- Oracles are cells (claim cells spawned by cell-zero)
- Retry is graph rewrite, not state mutation
- Everything is a cell — uniform evaluation model
- Confluence makes the fusion work

fix(uniopbench): trust agent's own test results and improve stop signals

The orchestrator was re-running test.py externally after the agent
finished, but on Hygon (non-NV) machines the external subprocess lacked
LD_LIBRARY_PATH that the agent had discovered and set, causing false
FAILED judgments.

Changes:
- Add parse_agent_test_results() to extract pass/fail from agent's
  trajectory log; fall back to external verification only when no
  agent results are found
- Propagate `export VAR=VALUE` from run_shell commands into os.environ
  so later subprocesses inherit agent-set env vars
- Update TASK.md with clear stop conditions: once correctness passes,
  run perf once then stop — no more unbounded optimization loops
- Make variants command conditional in user prompt (only shown when
  the operator's test.py actually supports --variants)
- Fix error message to use result dict instead of potentially undefined
  local variables

commit.message:
Fix sign out, portal infinite loading, and login redirect issues

Sign Out:
- Navigate to homepage after sign out
- Clear user/profile state immediately before calling supabase.auth.signOut()
- Handle SIGNED_OUT event explicitly in onAuthStateChange to avoid
  race condition with async profile fetch

Portal Infinite Loading:
- ProtectedRoute was showing spinner forever when profile was null
  after loading completed (e.g. missing profile row or RLS error)
- Now redirects to /login if user exists but profile is null

Login Redirect:
- Moved redirect logic from render-time to useEffect (React anti-pattern)
- Single useAuth() call instead of two separate calls
- Redirect fires reactively when profile loads after sign-in

Auth Context:
- Added error handling to fetchProfile (was silently swallowing errors)
- Added isMounted guard to prevent state updates after unmount
- Logs profile fetch errors to console for debugging

feat: PMAT-064/065 Q4K WMMA GEMM + FP16 dequant kernels (Refs PMAT-064)

PMAT-064: Q4K WMMA GEMM kernel — dequants Q4K→FP16 in SHMEM, uses
WMMA 16×16×16 tensor core tiles. Fixed 3 bugs: C store ×4 byte
multiplier, WMMA OOB writes (padded buffer), D2D race (stream sync).
Correct (max_err ≤ 0.000007 vs cuBLAS). 16x slow (naive, foundation).

PMAT-065: Q4KDequantFp16Kernel — direct Q4K→FP16 dequant (no F32
intermediate). Half output bandwidth vs Q4KDequantKernel.

Removed debug eprintln from tensor core GEMM test.

feat(policy): integrate PolicyDecisionLog into PolicyEngine

Wire the append-only PolicyDecisionLog (introduced in a86b4c31) into
the PolicyEngine so every authorize() call produces a forensic record.

config.rs:
- Add decision_log field (DecisionLogConfig) to SafetyConfig with
  default initialization, making the log configurable via the standard
  safety config surface

policy.rs — PolicyEngine integration:
- Add decision_log field (PolicyDecisionLog) to PolicyEngine
- Add with_decision_log_config() builder for custom config
- Add decision_log()/decision_log_mut() accessors for querying and
  export
- Split authorize() into evaluate_authorization() (pure logic) and
  record_to_decision_log() (side-effect), so the decision is computed
  before recording
- record_to_decision_log() maps PolicyDecision variants to
  DecisionOutcome, captures action/actor/surface/pane_id/rule_id/
  reason/rules_evaluated count with millisecond timestamps

Tests (8 new integration tests):
- decision_log_records_allow: verify allow decisions are logged
- decision_log_records_deny: verify deny decisions are logged
- decision_log_records_require_approval: verify approval requests
- decision_log_accumulates_multiple_decisions: 4 decisions, check counts
- decision_log_captures_rule_id: verify rule attribution
- decision_log_captures_pane_id_and_surface: verify input metadata
- decision_log_respects_config_skip_allows: verify record_allows=false
- decision_log_records_rules_evaluated_count: verify gate counting
- decision_log_export_json: verify JSON export format

perf: non-blocking TP/SL exits — monitor loop no longer stalls

TP1, TP2, Stop Loss, and Break-Even exit actions now run in the
background via executeExitAction(). The monitor loop continues
checking other pools immediately instead of waiting 30-60s for
each exit to confirm on-chain. activeTpSlActions set prevents
double-execution, cleaned up in finally block.

- 6 config_loader async functions wrapped in asyncio.to_thread
